tiny_practice.action: Hold an object in your hand and imagine God transforming it as a symbol of His power and your potential

two_step.reflect_question: What fears are preventing you from fully trusting God's calling in your life?

options.response: Write down your strengths and God's promises of empowerment
Practice affirming scriptures about God's support and presence
List past challenges you've overcome with God's help

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a staff/rod transforming into a living creature, representing God's miraculous power

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of God's empowerment changed after reading this chapter?
options: I see God uses ordinary people for extraordinary purposes
I recognize God provides supernatural signs to confirm His calling
I understand God partners with our weaknesses to demonstrate His strength
I'm more aware that obedience requires courage and trust

three_choice.question: Which aspect of divine transformation resonates most?

choices.expansion: Symbol of potential power lying dormant within ordinary objects
Divine willingness to provide tangible evidence of supernatural intervention

socratic_nudge.question: What transformative power might lie hidden in the ordinary objects of your own life that you've yet to recognize?

moral_slider.question: How do personal limitations interact with divine purpose?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of divine calling shifted?

thematic_summaries.title: Divine Empowerment and Validation
thematic_summaries.summary: God provides Moses with miraculous signs to validate his divine calling and mission. These supernatural demonstrations serve as proof of God's power and Moses' appointed leadership role in delivering Israel.

thematic_summaries.title: Overcoming Human Inadequacy
thematic_summaries.summary: Moses' initial reluctance and perceived limitations are transformed by God's supernatural intervention. Divine power compensates for human weakness, demonstrating that God equips those He calls.

reevaluation.question: How does God's response to Moses' hesitation reveal divine perspective on human capability?
options: God values willingness over perceived personal competence
Supernatural power can overcome human limitations
Obedience matters more than personal qualifications
